source: Learning bash with Rocky - Loops: The for loop over a list and the select menu

questions.q: What is the syntax of a for loop over a list?
options: for variable in list; do ... done
questions.why: for assigns each element of the list to the variable in turn.

questions.q: How do you loop with for over the output of a command?
options: Use a sub-execution after in, e.g. for f in $(ls -d /tmp/*)
questions.why: Any command producing a list can follow in via $(...).

questions.q: To make a for loop read a file line by line, what must you change?
options: Set IFS to $'\t\n' (remove the space) so it splits on lines, not words
Nothing -- it reads lines by default
questions.why: By default IFS also splits on spaces; removing the space makes each line one element.

questions.q: What does the select structure display?
options: A numbered menu, assigning the chosen item to a variable
questions.why: select shows numbered choices and reads the user's pick.

questions.q: In select, which variable is the prompt and which holds the chosen number?
options: PS3 is the prompt; REPLY holds the entered number
questions.why: PS3 sets the input prompt; REPLY returns the number chosen.

questions.q: How do you leave a select loop?
questions.why: select needs an explicit break to exit.
